task_completion.description: Log into FreeMED using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the FreeMED interface indicating a successful login (e.g., dashboard view or user profile showing admin).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Appointment Templates or Categories configuration section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the FreeMED interface for managing Appointment Templates or Categories.
Screenshot shows 'Diabetes Education' typed into the Name or Description input field for the new appointment template.
Screenshot shows '45' entered into the Duration or Length input field.

task_completion.description: Select a visible color for the appointment category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a color selected from a picker or a color code entered in the relevant color configuration field.

task_completion.description: Save the configuration and ensure it is available for scheduling.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the saved 'Diabetes Education' category appearing in the list of existing appointment templates, or visible as an option in the front-desk scheduling interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeMED web interface rather than using backend shortcuts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the FreeMED application UI, not a terminal, API client, or direct database manipulation tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the result in a text editor or static HTML page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and success states are shown within the live FreeMED web application UI, with correct styling and browser context.

integrity.description: The final saved state appears as a genuine system record.
integrity.visual_evidence: The newly created category appears integrated into the FreeMED data tables or scheduling dropdowns, matching the standard UI layout of the application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected name for the appointment category is exactly 'Diabetes Education' and the expected duration is exactly 45. Note: The FreeMED system does not actually have a 'code' field for Appointment Templates, making the requested code 'DBED' impossible to satisfy. Do not look for or penalize the agent for omitting the code.
